steps.title: Identify the GCF of all terms
steps.content: The expression has two terms: $7x^3$ and $56x^2$. The GCF of the coefficients 7 and 56 is 7; the GCF of the variables $x^3$ and $x^2$ is $x^2$. Therefore, the overall GCF is $7x^2$.

steps.title: Factor out the GCF
steps.content:
Divide each term by $7x^2$:
$$7x^3 \div 7x^2 = x$$
$$56x^2 \div 7x^2 = 8$$

Therefore: $$7x^3 + 56x^2 = 7x^2(x + 8)$$

That matches choice C — C is correct.
